title: Spring Testing & Testcontainers
learningObjectives: Explain Spring Testing & Testcontainers with phase-10 depth and clarity
Implement Java examples for Spring Testing & Testcontainers with production awareness
Identify failure risks and mitigation strategies
Reason about trade-offs under realistic constraints
Design or optimize solutions with maintainability in mind
Present interview answers with concrete evidence and structure

sections.title: Why Spring Testing & Testcontainers matters
sections.content:
Spring Testing & Testcontainers is a high-value Day 86 topic in Phase 10 (testing, performance diagnostics, and interview readiness).

At this stage, interviewers evaluate whether you can connect implementation details with reliability, scalability, and maintainability outcomes.

What strong answers include:
1. precise concept framing,
2. operational implications,
3. one realistic failure pattern,
4. one practical mitigation.

This topic is not just about code that compiles. It is about decisions that survive production traffic and team ownership.

sections.title: Theory and Internals - Spring Testing & Testcontainers
sections.content:
### Day 86: Spring Testing & Testcontainers
Phase context: testing, performance diagnostics, and interview readiness.

### What to understand deeply
- mechanism and boundaries
- runtime behavior and cost
- operational failure modes
- trade-offs vs alternatives

### Implementation workflow
1. Build a clear baseline solution.
2. Validate edge/failure paths.
3. Measure impact (latency, memory, throughput, or complexity).
4. Refactor for readability and maintainability.

### Interview framing template
- "The problem this solves is..."
- "I chose this approach because..."
- "The key trade-off is..."
- "A failure case is... and mitigation is..."

### Production perspective
For Spring Testing & Testcontainers, quality is judged by correctness + resilience + debuggability, not just API usage.

### Satyverse — deeper path (theory → practice)
**JUnit 5** — parameterized tests, nested, lifecycle; extensions for timing and resources.

**AssertJ** fluent assertions — readable failures; avoid assertTrue mega-expressions.

**Test naming** — behaviour-focused (`shouldRejectWhenBalanceNegative`) not method names only.

**Coverage:** line coverage is weak signal; branch and mutation testing find blind spots.

sections.title: Basic Walkthrough
sections.description: @SpringBootTest idea: load full context — heavy; prefer slice tests when possible.

sections.title: Intermediate Implementation
sections.description: Testcontainers: start real Postgres in Docker for integration tests.

sections.title: Common Pitfalls
items: Over-mocking tests and missing integration confidence.
Analyzing dumps/logs without hypothesis and scope.
Practicing interview questions without structured debrief.
Focusing on syntax over communication and reasoning quality.

sections.title: Exercise - Spring Testing & Testcontainers Applied Scenario
sections.problem:
Implement a realistic solution for **Spring Testing & Testcontainers**.

Requirements:
1. Define assumptions and constraints first.
2. Include one failure-path handling strategy.
3. Provide one improvement over baseline.
4. State complexity or runtime impact.
5. Keep implementation review-friendly.
hints: Add one observability/debug aid in output or comments.

sections.title: Interview Drill

conceptual.question: What is @SpringBootTest?
conceptual.answer: Full application context integration test—slower broad scope.

conceptual.question: What is @WebMvcTest?
conceptual.answer: Slice only web layer MockMvc controllers—mock beans fast.

conceptual.question: What is @DataJpaTest?
conceptual.answer: JPA slice embedded database default—repository tests.

conceptual.question: What is Testcontainers?
conceptual.answer: Docker containers for real DB Kafka etc.—integration fidelity—CI requirements Docker.

conceptual.question: What is @DynamicPropertySource Testcontainers?
conceptual.answer: Inject random ports JDBC URL into Spring context dynamic.

conceptual.question: What is @MockBean vs @MockitoBean?
conceptual.answer: Spring Boot 3.4+ MockitoBean refined—mock injection clarity evolving.

conceptual.question: What is slice vs full context trade-off?
What is WireMock?
conceptual.answer: Stub external HTTP services—deterministic tests no real third party.

conceptual.question: What is Awaitility async tests?
conceptual.answer: Poll until condition timeout—reactive async flows.

conceptual.question: What is @Nested JUnit 5?
conceptual.answer: Organize test classes hierarchical structure readability.

conceptual.question: What is testcontainers reuse Ryuk?
conceptual.answer: Resource reaper container—speed local dev mode experimental.

seniorScenario.question: Testcontainers CI slow flaky docker?
seniorScenario.answer: Ryuk permissions daemon rootless docker—stabilize runners.

seniorScenario.question: Shared test database pollution?
seniorScenario.answer: Isolation per test class transactions random schema Testcontainers fresh.

seniorScenario.question: Full Spring context every unit test suite 30 min?
seniorScenario.answer: Refactor slices architecture faster feedback loops.

seniorScenario.question: Production-like data volume tests?
seniorScenario.answer: Subset generation anonymized GDPR compliant performance tests separate env.

seniorScenario.question: Contract drift consumer provider?
seniorScenario.answer: Spring Cloud Contract Pact pipeline gates releases.

seniorScenario.question: Flaky docker port collisions parallel CI?
seniorScenario.answer: Random ports dynamic properties serialization race conditions rare.

wrongAnswers: @DataJpaTest loads the entire Spring Boot application always.
Testcontainers runs containers in-memory without Docker.
MockMvc requires a running servlet container always.
WireMock can only stub HTTPS with a valid public certificate always.
Integration tests should never use transactions or rollbacks.

sections.title: Cheatsheet
sections.content:
| Area | Phase 10 Expectation | Quick Rule | Gotcha |
|---|---|---|---|
| Framing | Explain intent + constraints | Start from problem | API-only answer |
| Design | State trade-off explicitly | Compare alternatives | One-solution bias |
| Runtime | Mention cost impact | Time + space/ops | No performance reasoning |
| Failure | Name one incident pattern | Mitigation path | Happy-path only thinking |
| Communication | Be concise + concrete | Use one example | Generic buzzwords |
